pick.rationale: St. John's holds a commanding advantage as a top-20 team ranked 18th in Torvik and 20th in KenPom compared to Northern Iowa's 70th/71st rankings. The Red Storm's Big East pedigree combined with their elite defensive efficiency (12th nationally at adjD: 95.1) and strong offensive rating (119.9, 46th nationally) create a clear mismatch against a Missouri Valley team whose offense ranks 153rd nationally. With nearly double the efficiency margin (+24.8 vs +11.86) and a superior 27-6 record versus Northern Iowa's 23-12, St. John's has the talent and system depth to handle the 12-seed comfortably in this Round of 64 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Analyzed CBB ratings and matchup context for St. John's vs Northern Iowa
reasoningStep.summary: Compared advanced metrics between both teams to identify significant quality gap favoring the 5-seed
reasoningStep.evidence: St. John's ranked 18th/20th in Torvik/KenPom with +24.8 AdjEM vs Northern Iowa's 70th/71st rankings with +11.86 AdjEM; St. John's defensive efficiency ranked 12th nationally while Northern Iowa's offense ranked 153rd; Big East conference strength significantly exceeds Missouri Valley

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r64-east-3
Current game label: East 5/12
Round: Round of 64
Resolved teams: St. John's (st-john-s) vs Northern Iowa (northern-iowa)
Prior committed picks: 6

I need to predict the winner of game r64-east-3, which is St. John's (st-john-s) vs Northern Iowa (northern-iowa). This is an East 5/12 matchup in the Round of 64.

Let me gather relevant information about both teams:
1. Get their CBB ratings (Torvik and KenPom)
2. Look for any recent news or injury concerns
3. Consider their conference pedigrees and tournament experience

Let me start by looking up their ratings.

{
  "toolName": "lookup_cbb_ratings",
  "result": [
    {
      "teamId": "st-john-s",
      "teamName": "St. John's",
      "torvik": {
        "rank": 18,
        "team": "St. John's",
        "conf": "BE",
        "record": "27-6",
        "adjoe": 119.42892421267956,
        "oe Rank": 47,
        "adjde": 94.83902875441956,
        "de Rank": 12
      },
      "kenpom": {
        "rank": 20,
        "team": "St. John's",
        "conf": "BE",
        "record": "27-6",
        "adj_em": 24.8,
        "adj_o": 119.9,
        "adj_o_rank": 46,
        "adj_d": 95.1
      }
    },
    {
      "teamId": "northern-iowa",
      "teamName": "Northern Iowa",
      "torvik": {
        "rank": 70,
        "team": "Northern Iowa",
        "conf": "MVC",
        "record": "23-12",
        "adjoe": 109.659067210004,
        "oe Rank": 151,
        "adjde": 98.53563210898326,
        "de Rank": 26
      },
      "kenpom": {
        "rank": 71,
        "team": "Northern Iowa",
        "conf": "MVC",
        "record": "23-12",
        "adj_em": 11.86,
        "adj_o": 110.1,
        "adj_o_rank": 153,
        "adj_d": 98.2
      }
    }
  ]
}

Great! Now I have the ratings for both teams:

St. John's:
- Torvik rank: 18
- KenPom rank: 20
- Record: 27-6
- Conference: Big East (BE)
- AdjO: ~119.4-119.9 (ranked 46-47th nationally)
- AdjD: ~94.8-95.1 (ranked 12th nationally)
- AdjEM: +24.8

Northern Iowa:
- Torvik rank: 70
- KenPom rank: 71
- Record: 23-12
- Conference: Missouri Valley (MVC)
- AdjO: ~109.6-110.1 (ranked 151st nationally)
- AdjD: ~98.2-98.5 (ranked 26th nationally)
- AdjEM: +11.86

The gap is significant - St. John's is ranked in the top 20 by both systems, while Northern Iowa is around 70th. St. John's has a much better record (27-6 vs 23-12), plays in a stronger conference (Big East vs MVC), and has superior offensive efficiency (nearly 10 points per 100 possessions better). Their defensive efficiency is also slightly better.

This is a classic 5/12 matchup where the higher seed should win. Let me check if there are any injury concerns or recent news that might affect this prediction.

modelTrace.content:
The web searches are all failing with 403 errors. This is a consistent issue. Let me try one more search with a different query, or I may need to proceed with the information I have.

Based on the ratings data I already have:
- St. John's is ranked 18th in Torvik and 20th in KenPom
- Northern Iowa is ranked 70th in both systems
- St. John's has a 27-6 record vs Northern Iowa's 23-12
- St. John's plays in the Big East (stronger conference) vs MVC
- St. John's offensive efficiency is ~10 points per 100 possessions better
- St. John's defensive efficiency is slightly better as well
- St. John's AdjEM (+24.8) is double that of Northern Iowa (+11.86)

This is a clear mismatch where the 5-seed should beat the 12-seed. The gap in rankings alone (18-20 vs 70-71) is substantial. In March Madness, 5/12 matchups go to the higher seed about 75% of the time historically.

I don't have any injury concerns from the failed searches, so I'll proceed with confidence in St. John's based on the overwhelming metrics advantage.

modelTrace.content:
All web searches are failing with 403 errors. I have enough information from the CBB ratings to make a confident pick. The data shows:

St. John's:
- Torvik rank: 18, KenPom rank: 20
- Record: 27-6
- Big East conference (Power 5)
- AdjO: ~119.5 (46th nationally)
- AdjD: ~95.0 (12th nationally)
- AdjEM: +24.8

Northern Iowa:
- Torvik rank: 70, KenPom rank: 71
- Record: 23-12
- Missouri Valley Conference (mid-major)
- AdjO: ~110.0 (153rd nationally)
- AdjD: ~98.3 (26th nationally)
- AdjEM: +11.86

The gap is massive - St. John's is ranked in the top 20 while Northern Iowa is around 70th. St. John's has nearly double the efficiency margin and plays in a much stronger conference. This is a classic 5/12 matchup where the higher seed should win comfortably.

I'll pick St. John's with high confidence based on these metrics.

Good, I have the ratings confirmed. Now let me make my pick based on this data:

St. John's is clearly superior across all metrics:
- 52 ranking positions better in Torvik (18 vs 70)
- 51 ranking positions better in KenPom (20 vs 71)
- Better record (27-6 vs 23-12)
- Much stronger conference (Big East vs MVC)
- Nearly 10 points per 100 possessions better offensive efficiency
- Slightly better defensive efficiency
- Double the efficiency margin (+24.8 vs +11.86)

This is a straightforward 5/12 matchup where the higher seed should win. The gap in quality is substantial enough that I'm confident in St. John's winning. I'll set confidence around 75-78% given it's still a tournament game where anything can happen, but the metrics strongly favor St. John's.
